chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions on how to import Rheonics sensor project files, including configurations for viscosity, temperature, and density, into the Connected Components Workbench (CCW) software environment.
chunks.extracted_text:
1.2. Once in the CCW environment go to the “file” tab and select Import project. Rheonics provides 2 example files for the Ethernet/IP; example 4 A file (Viscosity, Temperature, and Density) and 4B (All the parameters shown in Figure 2)

Figure 3. Selecting ccwarc file

1.4. The Ethernet settings can be verified by selecting the Ethernet option. The configuration of the IP address can be changed if needed.
key_facts: Rheonics provides two example files for Ethernet/IP integration, one (4A) for Viscosity, Temperature, and Density parameters, and another (4B) for all parameters.
Project configurations for Rheonics sensors are stored in .ccwarc files.
The software environment used for project import and configuration is Connected Components Workbench (CCW).
Ethernet settings, including the IP address, can be verified and changed after importing a project.
diagram_labels: Design, configure, program, visualize and maintain,

1.5. Configure the IP Address of the Rheonics SRV sensor in the network, go to the “Prog1” by double-clicking. The Ladder program will be displayed. Next, follow these steps:
key_facts: The document details how to configure the Ethernet settings for a Micro820 controller.
The project is named 'RheonicsSRV_eval_4A' and is managed in CCWARC software.
The Micro820 controller's general information, including vendor (Allen-Bradley) and catalog ID (2080-LC20-20QBB), is displayed.
Ethernet port state is enabled with auto-negotiation for speed and duplex mode.
A static IP address configuration (192.168.8.134, subnet mask 255.255.255.0, gateway 192.168.8.1) is shown as selected for the controller.
The configuration aims to set the IP address for a Rheonics SRV sensor in the network.
Accessing the ladder program involves double-clicking 'Prog1' in the project organizer.
diagram_labels: Obtain IP address automatically using DHCP
Figure 4. Main page for CCWARC project.

1.7. Build the program and download it in the PLC.

Terminal Application Language:
Build (button)

Figure 8. Build Program process

1.8. Select the option Download with Values.
key_facts: Ethernet/IP configuration involves a ladder program within the Micro820 PLC environment.
The CIP Destination Path for the SRV sensor is configured within the A_TargetCfg.Path variable.
The IP address of the SRV sensor must be entered in the format '4,SRV IP Address', for example '4,192.168.8.133'.
The connection type for A_TargetCfg.CipC can be Unconnected (0) or Class3 (1).
After configuration, the program must be built and downloaded to the PLC.
The 'Download with Values' option should be selected when downloading the program to the PLC.
diagram_labels: TRUE: Close CIP connection upon n
warnings_notes: Note: For more information, check the 'Micro800 Programmable Controllers: Getting Started with CIP Client Messaging' guide.
Note: Remember to use the following format for the IP Address: '4,SRV IP Address'.

key_facts: The document guides the user through downloading a project named 'RheonicsSRV_eval_4A' to a Micro820 PLC.
The download process will overwrite any existing project content on the controller.
The controller will automatically switch to Program Mode to facilitate the download operation.
After a successful download, the user must transition to online mode to verify parameters.
Upon completion of the download, the user is prompted to change the controller to 'Remote Run' mode to execute the project.
The Ethernet port settings include an option for 'Auto-Negotiate Speed and Duplex Mode' for network configuration.
diagram_labels: Obtain IP address automatically using DHCP
warnings_notes: Warnings: The project in the controller will be overwritten with current project content.
Controller will be switched to Program Mode to perform the operation.
Download is complete. Change the controller to Remote Run to execute controller project?

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to check the online status of a system and verify the serial number of a Rheonics sensor within a PLC programming environment.
chunks.extracted_text:
1.10. Check for the green icon, it should state “Connected”, this will show whether the system is online and the program is reacting to the dataframe it is receiving.
Figure 11. Online Mode

    Q
    CtrlCfg (Input): A_CtrlCfg: False
    Status (Output): A_Status: False
    AppCfg (Input): A_AppCfgId: 1
    ResLeng (Output): A_DataLen: 35
    TargetCfg (Input): A_TargetCfg: 4.192.168.8...
    ReqData (Input): A_ReqData: 0
    ReqLeng (Input): A_ReqDataLen: 200
    ResData (Output): A_Data: 118

1.11. Check data displayed. Scroll-down the lateral bar until rug 4 is reached. There, verify that the serial number of the rheonics sensor is being read.
Figure 12. Serial Number Rheonics sensor SRV

1.12. This can also be checked by going to Local Variables and expanding the A_String parameter
key_facts: The 'Connected' icon indicates the system is online and the program is actively processing received dataframes.
The serial number of the Rheonics sensor can be verified by observing the 'A_String' parameter within the program's data display.
The 'A_String' parameter, which contains the sensor's serial number, can be found by navigating to 'Local Variables' and expanding it.

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates the display of Rheonics sensor parameters (viscosity, temperature, density, pressure) within a Rockwell Automation Micro820 PLC's Local Variable table and provides links to related Ethernet/IP integration resources.
chunks.extracted_text:
1.13. Parameters in the 4 A example are Viscosity, Temperature, and Density. The data should be displayed in the ladder program and also in the Local Variable table.

Resources:
*   Ethernet/IP Integration with Micro820-Getting Started (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000397129)
*   EthernetIP EDS file for Rheonics inline viscometer and density meter (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000393236)
*   Parameter List access over EthernetIP (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000393235)
key_facts: Viscosity, Temperature, and Density parameters are displayed in the ladder program and Local Variable table of a PLC.
The A_String parameter for a Rheonics SRV device is identified as 'RH BJBW-B00-0000 SRV'.

The page provides links to support resources for Ethernet/IP integration with Micro820 PLCs, including an EDS file for Rheonics inline viscometers and density meters, and parameter list access information.
